label: API consumption
title: External signal, normalized.
summary: Call a live vendor API and turn its response into a stable, useful interface.
detailSummary: This tool calls GitHub's public status endpoint, normalizes the vendor-specific response, and presents a small contract with timing and source evidence.
instructions: Review the normalized provider status and response time.
Open the JSON disclosure to inspect the stable contract returned to the interface.
proof: A live dependency can be normalized, timed, and inspected through a stable interface.

label: API publication
title: A versioned interface, published live.
summary: Select and call a public endpoint from a machine-readable contract.
detailSummary: The endpoint selector is populated from the site's published contract. The same interface can resolve to static GitHub Pages JSON or an edge adapter without changing the tool.
instructions: Choose a versioned endpoint from the contract-driven selector.
Inspect the status, runtime mode, timing, and returned JSON.
inputs: Optional configured edge API base URL
proof: A machine-readable contract can drive both publication and consumption.

label: Local language model
title: Describe the friction. Let the browser find the fit.
summary: Use a browser-local language model to match an operational problem to a capability.
detailSummary: A quantized sentence-embedding model runs in the visitor's browser. The text stays on the device; the resulting vector is compared with the published capability contract.
instructions: Describe the operational friction in plain language.
Review the strongest capability match, confidence, and browser runtime.
inputs: A plain-language description entered by the visitor
proof: Portmason Collections™ can support an executable workspace, not only static content.

label: Performance evidence
title: Built to stay light—and measured.
summary: Inspect this page's transfer evidence and compare another public homepage using the same test.
detailSummary: The tool combines browser-observed transfer data with an optional Google PageSpeed comparison, making performance claims inspectable instead of decorative.
instructions: Review the data this browser observed loading for the current page.
Run the same desktop PageSpeed page-weight and visual-load check against both sites.
outputs: Side-by-side page weight and visual-load duration
Comparison with a typical desktop homepage
proof: Operational claims can be measured and exposed as evidence in the same interface.
